zotero.abstractNote: Quality-Diversity (QD) algorithms have recently emerged as a powerful tool for finding a diverse set of high-performing solutions. However, their application to real-world robotics is often hindered by the high sample complexity of the underlying evolutionary search. In this paper, we propose a model-based approach to QD search that leverages learned forward models to accelerate the discovery of diverse and high-performing behaviors. By using the model to perform imaginary rollouts, we can evaluate candidate solutions without the need for costly real-world interactions. We demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach on a variety of simulated robotic tasks, showing that it significantly improves sample efficiency compared to model-free QD methods.
zotero.conferenceName: International Conference on Intelligent Robots and Systems (IROS)
zotero.proceedingsTitle: International Conference on Intelligent Robots and Systems (IROS)
